title: Illness
description: Hello lovely readers of this newsletter, I hope you're as excited as me for Berlin's summer! There's always so much to do, so many fun events, so many people to see. My weekends are always booked out completely and I end up riding this social high where I've got all these hobbies and cool shit going on and it feels amazing.

Except when it doesn't! I, like all other people, get sick sometimes. I caught a cold and stayed at home for two days recovering from it. But then I just decided I was fine. This is because it was the weekend and I had plans! So I went up and met with two groups of friends and promptly spread the germs onto my friends and got them sick too.

I'm a fucking idiot! This was totally foreseeable. I may have had energy and it wasn't that bad at all, but I was coughing and worst of all, my voice sounded different because of the congestion. So everyone knew. This has happened before and I still haven't learned my lesson. The next days I spent living in guilt because I spread the cold and in order to atone for my sins and hopefully make myself better, I am laying out this story on my website for everyone to see.

Dear reader, just know that I'll not do this again. All I have to do is defeat FOMO. If I'm able to stay at home and play video games (something I actively want to do) instead of not letting down a friend and doing some social engagement, then I have won. I'm hoping that as I get older, defeating FOMO will get easier.

In website news, people have started using the upload image feature on the homepage. I've moved it to the very top in order to encourage more! Recently I walked through my hallway and saw just a random baby on the screen. I wonder who it is.

entries.title: Angeline Era
description: I really enjoyed playing this very artsy little action game. In the game you can fight by just walking into monsters which is actually surprisingly deep, gameplay-wise. How to proceed to the next area involves some puzzle solving and tactics, and each area is nice and bite-sized and there's always a nice reward. It's a lot of fun! There are great boss battles, and it never really gets boring.

It's got the same PS1-era graphics as Anodyne 2 (this developer's previous game which I also really loved) and honestly I love it. It's such a vibe. We are living in the time period where these graphics are cool again, like we had 10 years ago with pixel art. Great. The vibes in general are excellent, and made better by another excellent soundtrack.

The story is very high-concept, with a lot of floaty dialogue about religion and magic realism that is honestly impossible to follow. By the end I was skipping text because honestly I had no clue what was going on. And the ending is just completely baffling, to the point where I was happy when it finally ended. But I did make peace with the story. I decided to just enjoy the vibe of the writing rather than the content and that worked pretty well.

In the game, you have this button which searches the area you're in for secrets, which means that there is a general sense of there being a million things to find! To the game's credit, it's always clear where to look for secrets, but I did feel quite aimless when exploring the world. Only about halfway through when I found a map which told me how to finish the game that I stopped feeling this sense of being overwhelmed. I spent a lot of time just randomly finding levels and completing them, feeling like I was missing important content (I didn't find the blacksmith until right at the end).
I did really enjoy this game and I do really recommend it.

entries.title: Street Fighter III: 3rd Strike
description: My colleague Rüdiger set up a retro gaming console with tons of arcade games at the office and I've been spending my lunch breaks playing this game, allegedly one of the best fighting games ever. I was attracted to it thanks to its incredible soundtrack which I've already been enjoying for years.

The one thing stopping me from getting into fighting games was time, but now I have some, 20 minutes each day at work. At the time of writing, I've already learned how to input most of Chun-Li's moves! I'm just about beating my colleagues and have a long journey ahead of me but I really want to beat at least the arcade mode.

entries.title: Dracula
description: The close up shots of his face are hilarious. It's great. I really enjoy the female characters too. Definitely worth a watch but I preferred Nosferatu.

entries.title: Gay Propaganda 4.0 + Sissy Smut
description: A collection of 18 short gay films at Freiluftkino Kreuzberg. I don't think the hundreds of queer party people were expecting to watch so much video art! Bleach featured in like 3 of them, which for me is a bit much.

entries.title: Twilight
description: Leo and I watched this for science, and thoroughly enjoyed it. It is genuinely hilarious. Edward Cullen is so widerlich in every way but whatever we were still cheering for Bella. Incredible to think how this influenced so many straight women back then.
entries.metadata: Release: 2008, for the spoiler discussion please visit the films page!

entries.title: Frankenstein
description: So fun to watch these old horror films. Apparently back then they were scary as fuck! I love how the director is clearly gay because he made Frankenstein hot and smoulder into the camera a lot. The monster is great.
entries.metadata: Release: 1931, for the spoiler discussion please visit the films page!

entries.title: Ray of Light
description: Really enjoyed discovering this classic album from Madonna. It's so great that she has so much material that I can just get back into her music at different points in my life whenever I need. I love this album's positive vibe, and I love its 90's electronica sound. I also feel like the album is a big day-night cycle. You've got the daytime songs like 'Ray of Light', the sunset song 'Sky Fits Heaven', the night time song 'Frozen' and the dawn song 'Mer Girl. All are great in different ways.
entries.metadata: Artist: Madonna, Release: 1998, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the albums page!

entries.title: Wii Sports Resort
description: Nintendo are continuing to prioritise soundtracks with a Mii theme and honestly fair enough, I'm not tired of it. This continues the mid-00s frutiger aero vibe that I enjoy so much so bring it on. That Title theme alone, great. That being said, this soundtrack doesn't make for a great listening experience on its own. You can add a few of these tracks to your playlists and that's honestly enough.
entries.metadata: Platform: Wii, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the nintendo soundtracks page!

entries.title: Mario Kart World
description: Oh damn!!! This is what this app was made for! Finally they have started to release music from this incredible soundtrack.

For those of you who don't know, this soundtrack consists of 300+ incredible jazz fusion arrangements of all your favourite music from across mario and mario kart history. The amount of work and love that went into it is insane. Driving around free roam and just being bombarded with new remixes of nostalgic bangers is just a total joy. The first time hearing some of these got me so excited. There's a lot to say about this soundtrack so buckle up!

In this first batch of tracks we get all the music from the main 30 racetracks, half of which are brand new. Listening to them here (without SFX from youtube videos which record from gameplay!!) has given me a new appreciation for some of the ones that didn't stick out at first, like 'Dry Bones Burnout', 'Crown City', 'Salty Salty Speedway' and 'Dandelion Depths'. Then there's the retro tracks that they saved, like 'Sky-High Sundae' and 'Wario's Galleon' which sound much much nicer now. You've got the bombastic 'Toad's Factory', the salsa-flavoured 'Mario Circuit' (love it) and of course the legendary 'Peach Beach' is back. I just love this song and they finally did it justice after the horrible Mario Kart 8 Deluxe version. I do feel like 'Koopa Troopa Beach' in comparison ain't great though.

They also give us the intro music as you approach the racetrack, and most make the listening experience not great, but I will shoutout the amazing build up to 'Dino Dino Jungle', and I also enjoy the intense 'Airship Fortress' one. The incoming salsa of 'Mario Circuit' is also iconic. They really leaned into the latin roots of the Super Mario Kart!

We also need to talk about Rainbow Road. I honestly think, despite being so ambitious, it's a bit much. It does have that Rainbow Road magic but it feels a bit like they're trying too hard. I think it sounds kinda cheesy, especially that space station part. That being said, the best part of the track by far is the build up in the 'To Rainbow Road' section. Holy shit that gets me so hyped up. The first time playing it? Huge.

But the real magic of this game is the free roam music. There's so much of it and it plays at random in the game, so these tracks are rare. Having them all laid out in the app like this is a dream. Especially since there are super rare tracks which don't appear in free roam, and only in the normal races between tracks. I can't wait to hear them all without SFX! They are currently updating this over time.

I died when I heard SNES Rainbow Road for the first time. It's a bona fide disco remix of my favourite Rainbow Road and you know I got so excited about it that I made an extended dance mix that I DJ'd in Kitkat!! One of my greatest life achievements. That track goes so hard.

We've also got some great remixes which pay respects to the latin flavour of the original mario kart with the Brazilian style 'Choco Island' or 'Koopa Beach', or 'Donut Plains', there is so much to enjoy. That silky smooth 'Vanilla Lake' mix? Damn.

In an update they released the free roam tracks for Mario Galaxy, and there are some fucking bangers in there. 'Purple Comet Galaxy', already a favourite of mine, has been elevated to new heights. It's so good. Also loving 'Yoshi Star Galaxy' and 'Digga-Leg', two tracks which I only truly started appreciating when this soundtrack came out.
Next was Super Mario Bros 1,2 and 3. The map theme medley is by far away the best. It's perfect. Love how it switches between the areas. Love the strings.
entries.metadata: Platform: Switch 2, for a list of favourite tracks, please visit the nintendo soundtracks page!

entries.title: Aldous Harding
description: By many measures this was a bad concert: no stage banter, just played the songs like the record, and the merch was shit. But there's something special about her.
entries.metadata: Date: 21-06-2026, Venue: Huxley's Neue Welt, Berlin

entries.title: PinkPantheress
description: I've never seen this many people holding up phones for a concert! Lots of gen Z. It felt a little bit like watching TV with all the production and screens and choreography. More dancers than bandmembers like a real popstar! All in all though, I really enjoyed it because I love her songs.

entries.title: Dungeon Crawler Carl
description: This is a totally ridiculous scifi series about a dungeon and dragons style adventure featuring a white American dude bro and his ex-girlfriend's cat who become sapient. The humour is so painfully cringe and I rolled my eyes a million times reading it but I have to give it to this book for being so easy to read. Things just happen so quickly and it's so gripping that I found myself demolishing this whole series and actually loving it. It doesn't claim to be the best book ever but it's a lot of fun and it has a lot of anti-fascist and anti-capitalist themes, and the world-building is genuinely excellent. If you are a nerd who likes tabletop games you will fucking love this but also if you just fancy some light scifi reading, maybe give it a go.
entries.metadata: Author: Matt Dinniman, Release: 2020, for the spoiler discussion please visit the books page!

description: Painting day with Wolf. She looks kinda weird with the elongated neck and she didn't even appear in Mario Kart 64 but whatever
